subjectLine: Your senescent cells are blunting your workout gains 🏋️
borisTake: If your dasatinib+quercetin protocol isn't moving your fitness markers, IGFBP7 is the likely culprit — senescent cell burden is acting as a molecular governor on what training can actually do. I'll be tracking plasma IGFBP7 before and after the next senolytic cycle.
leadAnalysis: IGFBP7, a senescence-associated secreted protein, inversely predicts fitness gains from high-intensity training in older adults — meaning people carrying more senescent cells get less out of the same workout regardless of baseline fitness. Genetic knockout of IGFBP7 in mice markedly amplified training-induced gains across distinct protocols, while forced overexpression abolished those gains entirely, establishing clean mechanistic causality. This positions senescent cell clearance — dasatinib+quercetin, fisetin protocols — directly upstream of exercise responsiveness rather than as a parallel intervention. UK Biobank data confirmed the effect at population scale: lower circulating IGFBP7 tracks with lower all-cause mortality and reduced incident age-related disease, making this one of the most clinically actionable senescence papers in recent memory.
oneNumber: 1,275 | Adults in the BLSA longitudinal study where organ-specific senescence signatures tracked their own disease domains — immune cell senescence now predicts future diabetes onset

papers.title: IGFBP7 Is a Circulating Brake on Exercise Adaptation Linked to Senescence
papers.summary: Plasma IGFBP7 — a SASP protein secreted by senescent cells — inversely predicted fitness gains after one year of HIIT in older adults. Mouse knockout amplified training gains across protocols; overexpression abolished them. UK Biobank confirms lower IGFBP7 = lower mortality + fewer age-related diseases. Senolytics may unlock exercise responsiveness as a direct mechanism.

papers.title: ANKRD1 Sustains a Neurogenic Bone Marrow Niche and Counters Cognitive Aging
papers.summary: A distinct subpopulation of skull bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells generates neurons for the adult brain. Viral gene therapy driving ANKRD1 expression in these cells — delivered systemically with a tissue-specific promoter — boosted neurogenesis in aged mice and improved cognitive function. First demonstration that a bone marrow niche outside the brain is a viable target for cognitive aging.

papers.title: SASP-Dependent Paracrine Spreading of Senescence Between Human Brain Cell Types
papers.summary: Mapped the directionality and cell-type specificity of senescence spreading across astrocytes, microglia, endothelial cells, oligodendrocytes, and neurons in vitro. Some cell types can both spread and receive secondary senescence; others only receive. Key SASP ligand-receptor pairs were identified and pharmacologically blocked. A practical target list for brain-specific senomorphic interventions — with the noted caveat that blocking senescence spread may have a cancer risk tradeoff.

papers.title: Organ-Specific Senescence Signatures in Circulation Predict Tissue-Matched Disease (SenCat / BLSA)
papers.summary: Using 14 human cell-type senescence signatures from the SenCat catalog, researchers validated that circulating senescent proteins from specific organs predict conditions in those same organs across 1,275 BLSA and 997 InCHIANTI participants. Immune cell senescence signature predicts future diabetes onset. Pooled SAPs outperform generic aging markers. Lays the groundwork for a senescence burden blood test that maps organ-specific aging.

papers.title: Gut Bacteria Drive Age-Related Cognitive Decline via the Vagus Nerve (Stanford, Nature)
papers.summary: Young mice co-housed with old mice develop specific memory impairment after 1 month via microbiome transfer — but not under germ-free conditions. FMT from aged donors into germ-free young mice fully recapitulated cognitive deficits. Stanford researchers identified vagus nerve signaling as the anatomical pathway connecting gut bacterial changes to hippocampal memory encoding. Antibiotics reversed the effect. A specific Clostridiales species implicated as the likely driver.

papers.title: Zinc Protects Injured Arteries from Nuclear Dysmorphism and Accelerated Senescence
papers.summary: Femoral arteries damaged by angioplasty showed markedly dysmorphic vascular smooth muscle cell nuclei compared to controls — consistent with lamin A disruption and accelerated senescence. Rat carotid injury models replicated the finding. Zinc delivery to damaged cells partially restored nuclear morphology and reduced senescence markers. Links mechanical vascular injury directly to VSMC aging, and flags zinc as a candidate adjunct after catheter procedures.

papers.title: Mitochondrial Uptake in Mesenchymal Stromal Cells: Multiple Endocytic Pathways Confirmed
papers.summary: Isolated mitochondria from MSCs are internalized via multiple interchangeable endocytic pathways — making uptake robust rather than dependent on a single mechanism. Internalized mitochondria retained function, increased oxygen consumption rate, improved viability under stress, and promoted proliferation. Directly relevant to mitochondrial transplantation therapy design: redundancy in uptake pathways suggests delivery optimization should focus on mitochondrial quality rather than cell surface receptor targeting.
